executive_verdict.primary_failure_mode: No active cross-loop architecture break is visible; the main remaining weakness is that the marketing loop still has flat outcome movement despite healthy ownership, verification, and shared-learning boundaries.
executive_verdict.most_urgent_fix: Turn marketing learning into measurable Codeberg adoption movement without regressing the hardened live-topology and independent-verification boundaries.

audit_metadata.scheduler_inspection_method: direct openclaw cron list --json plus jobs.json comparison with explicit live-vs-persisted separation

owns: bounded timeout reruns and timeout tuning
artifact contract checks for code-backed shared-market reuse
syncing research artifacts into the git repo

findings.title: Marketing remains the highest-risk unresolved loop because outcome movement is still flat
findings.mechanism: The marketing audit still names distribution_and_message_to_primary_repo_conversion as the bottleneck, and the independent verifier still flags flat primary-repo adoption as an active watchpoint.
findings.root_cause: The marketing system is learning and reusing shared artifacts correctly, but current tactics have not yet produced measurable Codeberg adoption movement.
findings.recommended_fix: Keep replacing tactics and, if necessary, the marketing runtime itself until Codeberg movement becomes measurable instead of accepting measurement-pending as a steady state.

findings.title: Persisted disabled cron history still exists and must stay separated from live runtime claims
findings.mechanism: jobs.json still contains three disabled legacy jobs, while live Gateway cron currently shows 20 jobs with 0 live-disabled.
findings.root_cause: Historical retention is intentional, but it becomes dangerous if any watchdog or verifier collapses persisted history back into live topology reporting.
findings.recommended_fix: Keep all live-topology checks bound to openclaw cron list --json and report persisted disabled history only in separate persisted-history fields.

findings.title: No hidden self-certification is currently visible on the full-contract loops
findings.mechanism: Docs, marketing, health-monitor, loop-integrity, and architecture artifacts all show explicit verifier boundaries and current green status after direct reruns.
findings.root_cause: Recent verifier hardening is holding, but it remains a boundary that must keep being checked rather than assumed.
findings.recommended_fix: Preserve fail-closed verifier freshness and topology-coherence checks; do not loosen them just because the current state is green.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Convert marketing self-improvement into measurable Codeberg adoption movement
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: This is the highest-risk unresolved system weakness after the architecture-verification repairs held green.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: The system rewards real distribution outcomes instead of activity-only loop health.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Keep live-topology reporting fail-closed against persisted scheduler history
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: The historical disabled-job layer is still present in jobs.json and must never leak back into live-runtime claims.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Future watchdog artifacts remain trustworthy about the real active scheduler topology.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Preserve independent-verification freshness and topology-coherence gates across full-contract loops
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: The system is healthy partly because those gates are active; relaxing them would recreate hidden self-certification risk.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Repair loops continue to need fresh external signoff before being treated as healthy.

repairs_applied_this_run.change: No new material architecture repair was required in this run; the hardened topology and verifier boundaries remained green under direct rechecks.

independent_verification.summary: Fresh direct reruns and current peer artifacts confirm healthy topology ownership, green independent-verification boundaries, clean user-crontab ownership, and machine-verifiable shared market-intelligence reuse.

highest_risk_unresolved_issue.title: Marketing outcomes remain flat despite healthy loop discipline
highest_risk_unresolved_issue.why: Codeberg adoption is flat across the recent measurement window, so the active tactics are not earning real adoption movement yet.
